title: Customer Support Engineer
description: **Company Overview** KLA is a global leader in diversified electronics for the semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem. Virtually every electronic device in the world is produced using our technologies. No laptop, smartphone, wearable device, voice\-controlled gadget, flexible screen, VR device or smart car would have made it into your hands without us. KLA invents systems and solutions for the manufacturing of wafers and reticles, integrated circuits, packaging, printed circuit boards and flat panel displays. The innovative ideas and devices that are advancing humanity all begin with inspiration, research and development. KLA focuses more than average on innovation and we invest 15% of sales back into R\&D. Our expert teams of physicists, engineers, data scientists and problem\-solvers work together with the world’s leading technology providers to accelerate the delivery of tomorrow’s electronic devices. Life here is exciting and our teams thrive on tackling really hard problems. There is never a dull moment with us. **Group/Division** The KLA Services team headquartered in Milpitas, CA is our service organization that consists of Service Sales and Marketing, Spares Supply Chain management, Field Operations, Engineering, Product Training, and Technical Support. The KLA Services organization partners with our field teams and customers in all business sectors to maintain the high performance and productivity of our products through a flexible portfolio of services. Our comprehensive services include: proactive management of tools to identify and improve performance; expertise in optics, image processing and motion control with worldwide service engineers, 24/7 technical support teams and knowledge management systems; and an extensive parts network to ensure worldwide availability of parts. **Job Description/Preferred Qualifications** This role is primarily responsible for customer service activities associated with updating, troubleshooting, diagnosing and repairing of highly complex Surfscan equipment at customer sites. You will be representing KLA to the customer and will assume accountability for customer satisfaction with service. This role also assures operational quality of system equipment and will coordinate actions with customers to minimize down time. Responsibilities: * Evaluates, analyses, diagnoses and troubleshoots technical equipment problems at customer site. Ensures equipment enhances customer production. * Repairs and updates equipment at customer facility. Evaluates equipment to determine type and extent of problem. Resolves issues using standard procedures as well as system level problems which have multiple causes and for which no standard procedures exist. * Repairs of system level problems are based on the technical knowledge, education and training. * Conducts customer orientation and technical training on all aspects of equipment maintenance support for user applications. * Prepares field service reports on customer support activity and provides documentation to other supporting functions on re\-occurring problems. * Cross trains and assists other field service engineers as appropriate. Provides guidance and technical assistance to Installation Engineer on installations at customer sites or on installing/dismantling demonstration machines. **Preferred Experience / Skills** * Demonstrates expert level of analytical problem solving; Capability to determine root cause, provide relevant information and help to develop prevention techniques. * Expert abilities in troubleshooting software and electronic in all sub\-systems and systems. * Develops capabilities in use of all available diagnostic resources. System expert in all aspects of assigned equipment set. * Ability to address overall system issues (hardware, software, networking, automation, applications); ability to drive customer improvement programs, change order programs. * Proficient in general and factory automation on assigned equipment set. * Capability to work with Tech Support and Engineering in special projects and/or escalations * Proficiency in systems level repair of multiple or extremely complex products within a family is required. * Strong interpersonal/communication skills in understanding customer needs. * Ability to work under pressure and stringent timelines. * Ideally hold an Engineering qualification in Electrical or Electro / Mechanical discipline or have equivalent level of experience. **Minimum Qualifications** Engineering qualification in Electrical, Electro / Mechanical or similar discipline or have equivalent level of experience *We offer a competitive, family friendly total rewards package. We design our programs to reflect our commitment to an inclusive environment, while ensuring we provide benefits that meet the diverse needs of our employees.* *KLA is proud to be an equal opportunity employer* Be aware of potentially fraudulent job postings or suspicious recruiting activity by persons that are currently posing as KLA employees. KLA never asks for any financial compensation to be considered for an interview, to become an employee, or for equipment. Further, KLA does not work with any recruiters or third parties who charge such fees either directly or on behalf of KLA. Please ensure that you have searched KLA’s Careers website for legitimate job postings. KLA follows a recruiting process that involves multiple interviews in person or on video conferencing with our hiring managers. If you are concerned that a communication, an interview, an offer of employment, or that an employee is not legitimate, please send an email to talent.acquisition@kla.com to confirm the person you are communicating with is an employee. We take your privacy very seriously and confidentially handle your information.

title: Cyber Security Consultant
description: **Senior Cybersecurity Engineer – EDR \| SIEM \| Azure Security \| Vulnerability Management \| NIST** We are seeking a hands\-on Senior Cybersecurity Engineer to join an high\-impact engagement, where you will design, implement, and operate security controls across enterprise and cloud environments. This is a unique opportunity to work on a meaningful, security\-first programme with genuine operational responsibility. You will lead vulnerability remediation, optimise security tooling, and help embed security frameworks that drive real risk reduction. If you are an engineer who loves solving complex problems and wants to see your work make a tangible difference, this role is for you. **What You'll Do:** * Design, implement, and maintain cybersecurity controls across enterprise infrastructure and Azure cloud environments. * Operate and optimise EDR, SIEM, and vulnerability management platforms (Qualys, Tenable, etc.). * Lead vulnerability remediation activities, including end\-to\-end patch lifecycle management. * Support network security architecture, including firewalls, segmentation, and secure connectivity. * Develop, maintain, and enhance security standards, policies, and operational playbooks. * Partner with cross\-functional teams, cloud engineers, and third\-party suppliers to ensure secure design and configuration. * Support compliance with security frameworks including ISO 27001, NIST, and NIS2\. **What We're Looking For:** **Essential Qualifications:** * A minimum of 5 years of experience in cybersecurity engineering, infrastructure security, or systems administration within an enterprise setting. * Strong hands\-on experience with **EDR and SIEM platforms** . * Proven experience with **vulnerability management tools** (e.g., Qualys, Tenable) and patching processes. * Solid understanding of **networking, firewalls, segmentation, and Zero Trust principles** . * Practical knowledge of **Microsoft 365 and Azure security controls** . * Working knowledge of **Active Directory, IAM, and PAM** . * Experience working with security frameworks such as **ISO 27001, NIST, or NIS2** . * Ability to develop and maintain security policies, standards, and operational documentation. * Excellent analytical and problem\-solving abilities, with a track record of working independently in a fast\-paced environment. * Superb communication skills, with the ability to translate complex technical issues for non\-technical stakeholders. **Highly Preferred Qualifications:** * Incident response and alert triage experience. * Exposure to threat hunting, log analysis, or digital forensics. * Experience supporting audits, security assessments, or regulatory reporting. * Relevant certifications such as **CISSP, CISM, CySA\+, or Security\+** . **Our Tech Stack \& Environment:** You will be working with enterprise\-grade security tools and modern cloud platforms: * **Security Tooling:** EDR, SIEM, Qualys, Tenable * **Cloud \& Identity:** Microsoft 365, Azure security controls, Active Directory, IAM, PAM * **Network:** Firewalls, segmentation, secure connectivity * **Frameworks:** ISO 27001, NIST, NIS2, Zero Trust * **Culture:** Collaborative, autonomous, and focused on practical security outcomes rather than bureaucracy **Contract Details:** * **Duration:** 3 months initially (high likelihood of extension to 6\+ months) * **Location:** Dublin – Hybrid (2–3 days per week onsite) * **Start:** Immediate / short notice preferred

title: Data Centre Operative (Dublin, Ireland)
description: **Job Summary** To support daily operations, including equipment handling, light maintenance, monitoring, and inventory control. The Data Centre Operative needs to be committed to maintaining high standards of safety, security, and efficiency in a fast\-paced, mission\-critical environment. **Key Responsibilities** * Conduct regular site walk to identify alarms on equipment or abnormal conditions and escalate promptly. * Perform visual inspections and monitor key conditions (e.g., temperature/humidity where applicable), reporting unusual noise, overheating, faults, or irregular readings. * Accurately record tasks, readings, incidents, and outcomes in the required systems and provide clear updates to Operations, Technical teams, and management. * Maintain high housekeeping standards across assigned areas (including FOC, FOC Library, Stores, and Workshop), completing routine checks and following a “clean\-as\-you\-go” approach. * Support planned and unplanned outages, incidents, and emergency drills, following site procedures and escalation paths. * Escort, supervise, and monitor sub\-contractors /third\-party engineers on site to ensure adherence to site standards, safety rules, and access requirements during maintenance and related activities. * Receive and verify deliveries, ensure safe storage/handling of equipment, and arrange secure and compliant disposal of obsolete hardware/materials. * Follow strict access control procedures, adhere to manual handling and safe working practices, use PPE as required, and report hazards/near misses or unsafe conditions immediately. **Key Requirements** * Previous work experience in a hands\-on or operational role preferred. * Willingness to learn and follow procedures. * Good teamwork and communication skills. * Excellent verbal and written communications skills in English language. * Reliable, organised, and able to manage time effectively. * Strong organisational skills. * Ability to use computer applications effectively. Interpersonal Requirements * Committed to the delivery of excellent customer service. * Calm Manner, able to work under pressure. * Self\-motivated, ability to work on own initiative. * Punctual and reliable. * Good attention to detail. * A team player, able to work with colleagues and clients staff at all levels. Working environment entails * Walking job sites which have areas of uneven terrain. * Work at heights. * Regularly lift and/or move items and participate in group lifts. * Operate forklift at times. * Co\-ordinate body movements when using tools or equipment. * May require bending or twisting the body to access equipment while working. * Involves performing physical tasks all day. * Use hands to manipulate small wires and objects. * Push or pull objects into position. * Work in a noisy environment. * Work in confined spaces. **Education** Diploma in Electrical Engineering Others in Mechanical Engineering or Electrical Engineering \& Electronics

title: DevOps Engineer
description: Some companies use DevOps to ship apps faster. This team is using it to help reshape how future medicines are discovered. We’re working with a fast\-growing deep\-tech company operating at the intersection of AI, computational science, and pharmaceutical innovation. Their platform is tackling one of the biggest challenges in drug development,, using advanced simulation and crystal structure prediction technology to accelerate research that traditionally takes years. Now they’re looking for a DevOps Engineer who loves building systems from the ground up. Not just maintaining infrastructure… but architecting the engine that powers cutting\-edge scientific computing. **This is the kind of role where you’ll:** ⚙️ Build self\-hosted CI/CD systems engineers actually enjoy using 📦 Orchestrate containerised workloads across distributed infrastructure 📊 Design observability stacks that give real\-time visibility into complex systems 🛠 Own infrastructure\-as\-code and automation from day one 🧠 Work directly with highly technical R\&D teams and leadership on scaling architecture 🚀 Help create an environment where experimentation and innovation can move faster, safely They’re not looking for someone who waits for tickets to appear. They want someone curious, hands\-on, and excited by solving hard infrastructure problems in a highly technical environment. **What you’ll likely bring:** • Strong experience in DevOps / Platform Engineering / SRE • Confidence working with Docker, Podman, or container ecosystems • Experience building CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions, GitLab CI/CD, Jenkins, or similar • Hands\-on knowledge of observability and monitoring stacks (Prometheus, Grafana, alerting systems, etc.) • Infrastructure\-as\-Code experience with tools like Ansible • Strong Linux fundamentals and scripting skills • Python proficiency for automation and tooling • A self\-starter mindset with the ability to work closely with technical and non\-technical stakeholders alike **Why this role stands out:** This isn’t another “keep the lights on” DevOps role. You’ll be joining a highly innovative, award\-winning environment where infrastructure directly supports scientific breakthroughs, AI\-driven research, and next\-generation pharmaceutical technology. If you enjoy autonomy, technical depth, and building scalable systems that actually matter, this is worth a conversation.

title: DevOps Engineer

title: DevSecOps Engineer
description: We’re looking for a hands\-on Cloud Security Analyst with deep expertise in cloud\-native and container security. You will play a critical role in defining security standards, implementing hardened baselines, and enhancing detection and response capabilities—working closely with DevOps, Networking, and SOC teams to embed security into every layer of design and automation. 🧩 Key Responsibilities Strengthen and optimize cloud\-native security controls across IAM, network security (WAF, VPC), and data protection (KMS, Vault) Deploy and manage CSPM, CNAPP, and CWPP solutions to maintain continuous security posture across multi\-cloud and Kubernetes environments Integrate cloud telemetry (GCP Audit Logs, AWS CloudTrail, Azure Monitor, Kubernetes audit logs) into SIEM/SOAR platforms for centralized visibility Define and enforce cloud security architectures and guardrails aligned with Zero Trust and Least Privilege principles Develop and maintain Security\-as\-Code practices using Terraform, CloudFormation, or Bicep, including policy\-as\-code frameworks Own and enhance the security posture of containerized environments (GKE, AKS, EKS), including image, registry, and runtime security Harden Kubernetes clusters through RBAC, NetworkPolicies, Admission Controllers, and secure configurations Lead cloud security incident response activities including triage, containment, and forensic investigations Continuously monitor emerging threats, vulnerabilities, and attack vectors within cloud\-native ecosystems 🎯 Required Qualifications Experience 5\+ years in Information Security Minimum 3\+ years in Cloud Security At least 2\+ years working with Kubernetes / container security Cloud Expertise Hands\-on experience with at least two major cloud platforms (GCP and Azure preferred; AWS/OCI a plus) Container \& Kubernetes Security Strong understanding of Kubernetes security controls (RBAC, Secrets, Ingress, TLS) Familiarity with container runtime security and orchestration Technical Skills Proficiency in scripting (Python preferred; Bash or Go is a plus) Working knowledge of Infrastructure as Code (IaC) and automation Security Frameworks \& Compliance Knowledge of ISO 27001, SOC 2, NIST, and CIS Benchmarks Certifications (Preferred) CISSP CKS (Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist) GCP Professional Cloud Security Engineer Microsoft Azure Security Engineer / Cybersecurity Architect AWS Certified Security – Specialty Soft Skills Strong stakeholder management and ability to influence engineering teams Experience working in global or multi\-national environments ⭐ Nice to Have Experience securing microservices, APIs, and serverless architectures Background in penetration testing or red teaming (cloud/container focus) Experience in large\-scale SaaS, networking, or cloud\-first organizations Additional certifications across GCP, Azure, AWS, or OCI Kubernetes certifications such as CKA or advanced security\-focused credentials If interested kindly share your updated cv to remya\-saras@dyneits.com

description: About the Role We are looking for a driven and commercially minded Ecommerce Executive to join our growing apparel brands. You will take ownership of our Shopify stores, helping to drive revenue, optimise the customer journey, and support our digital growth strategy across multiple brands – Both B2B and DTC. This is a hands\-on, varied role suited to someone who thrives in a fast\-paced fashion environment and has a genuine passion for ecommerce and digital retail. **Role Overview** * **Job Title Ecommerce Executive** * **Industry Apparel / Fashion Retail** * **Location Dublin, Ireland (Hybrid considered)** * **Contract Full\-Time, Permanent** * **Experience 2–4 Years in Ecommerce** * **Reporting To Head of Digital / Ecommerce Manager** Key Responsibilities Shopify Store Management * Manage and maintain multiple Shopify stores across our apparel brands, ensuring optimal performance and user experience. * Oversee product uploads, descriptions, pricing, and imagery to keep catalogues accurate and on\-brand. * Configure and manage Shopify apps, plugins, and integrations (payments, fulfilment,reviews, etc.). * Monitor site health, resolve technical issues, and liaise with developers where needed. * Lead seasonal and promotional site updates, campaign launches, and new product drops. Trading \& Performance * Analyse and report on key ecommerce metrics including traffic, conversion rate, AOV, andrevenue. * Identify trading opportunities and recommend actions to drive sales and improve site performance. * Support the delivery of promotional calendars and ensure all commercial activity is live and accurate. * Work with the marketing team to align on\-site activity with paid, social, and email campaigns. Customer Experience \& CRO * Continuously review and optimise the customer journey across desktop and mobile. * Conduct A/B testing and make data\-driven recommendations to improve conversion. * Manage on\-site merchandising, collections, and search functionality. * Ensure the checkout experience is seamless, reducing basket abandonment. Brand \& Content * Ensure all brand stores maintain a consistent look, feel, and tone of voice. * Collaborate with creative teams on imagery, copy, and content requirements. * Support new brand or product launches end\-to\-end from a digital perspective. Social Media \& Digital Marketing * Plan and execute social media marketing activity across Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook to support brand awareness and drive traffic to our Shopify stores. * Create and manage paid digital advertising campaigns across Google Ads (Search,Shopping, Display) and Meta Ads, optimising for ROAS and conversion. * Monitor, analyse, and report on the performance of all paid and organic digital channels, making data\-led recommendations for improvement. * Work closely with the content and creative teams to develop social\-first assets that align with campaign objectives and brand guidelines. * Stay up to date with social commerce trends, platform updates, and emerging digital marketing opportunities relevant to the apparel industry. Requirements Essential * 2–4 years of hands\-on ecommerce experience, ideally within apparel or fashion retail. * Demonstrable experience managing Shopify stores at a brand or agency level. * Strong commercial acumen with the ability to translate data into actions. * Proficiency in Google Analytics (GA4\), Shopify Analytics, or similar tools. * Highly organised with excellent attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities. * Strong communication skills and ability to work cross\-functionally. * Strong eye for design, branding, and digital visual merchandising with an understanding of fashion retail aesthetics. * Experience creating and managing website layouts, promotional banners, and customer\-facing content within Shopify or similar ecommerce platforms. Desirable * Experience working across multiple brands or Shopify stores simultaneously. * Familiarity with email platforms such as Klaviyo and how they integrate with ecommerce. * Understanding of SEO best practices and on\-site optimisation. * Experience with Shopify * Knowledge of paid media channels and how they interact with onsite performance. * Proven track record running live selling events on TikTok, Instagram, or Facebook. * Experience with Google Ads (Search, Shopping) and Meta Ads Manager. * Experience using AI image generation tools (e.g. Merchd, Gemmini, ChatGPT, etc.) for commercial content creation. * Strong visual and creative judgement with exceptional attention to detail and product accuracy. * Understanding of prompt engineering and AI\-assisted content workflows within a retail or ecommerce environment. What We Offer * Generous staff discount across all our apparel brands. * A collaborative, creative team environment in a growing business. * Be at the forefront of using in\-house AI tools, working with the latest technology to enhance how we trade, create, and grow. Pay: €27,000\.00\-€35,000\.00 per year Benefits: * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

description: **We have job openings for Full Time work Monday \- Friday 40 hours per week. Please be available for these hours before applying for the position.** We have an amazing opportunity to hire a Childcare Practitioner and add to our excellent team at Parkside Childcare! We are one of south Dublin's most established childcare facilities, operating nearly 30 years! Experience: * Childcare: 3 years (preferred) Licence/Certification: * Level 5\+ Early Year Education or a letter from DCEDIY? (required) Work Location: In person

title: Engineer, PC Apps
description: Join the leader in entertainment innovation and help us design the future. At Dolby, science meets art, and high tech means more than computer code. As a member of the Dolby team, you’ll see and hear the results of your work everywhere, from movie theaters to smartphones. We continue to revolutionize how people create, deliver, and enjoy entertainment worldwide. To do that, we need the absolute best talent. We’re big enough to give you all the resources you need, and small enough so you can make a real difference and earn recognition for your work. We offer a collegial culture, challenging projects, and excellent compensation and benefits, not to mention a Flex Work approach that is truly flexible to support where, when, and how you do your best work. **The role** For Dolby’s Client Applications, you will contribute to building and maintaining consumer\-facing, client\-side applications used by millions of users worldwide. As a Software Engineer, you will work hands\-on on production code, implementing features within established architectures and engineering practices. You will work under guidance from more senior engineers while progressively building technical ownership of features and components. This role is intended for engineers who want to grow through real product work, learning a mature codebase and increasing responsibility over time. **What We Offer** * Work on consumer\-facing client applications with visible user impact. * A collaborative engineering environment that values quality, learning, and teamwork. * Modern development workflows, including automation and CI/CD. * Support for professional growth through mentoring, feedback, and hands\-on experience. * A clear path toward increasing technical ownership and senior responsibilities over time. **What You Will Do** * Implement application features and improvements using established architectures and coding standards. * Contribute to the development and maintenance of components across Dolby’s Client Applications portfolio. * Write clean, maintainable, and well tested code with support from more senior engineers. * Participate in code reviews and apply feedback to improve code quality and technical skills. * Collaborate with QA, product, and design partners to deliver reliable features and releases. * Contribute to automated testing and CI/CD pipelines as part of everyday development work. * Troubleshoot and resolve issues across application and platform layers. **Required** **What you bring** * Professional experience in software development. * Hands\-on experience with C\#. * Experience with Windows application frameworks (UWP, WinUI). * Basic understanding of C\+\+ or experience working with native components. * Experience building desktop or other client\-side applications. * Ability to write clean, maintainable code and work effectively within an existing codebase. * Willingness to learn, accept feedback, and grow technical ownership over time. * Clear communication skills and a collaborative mindset. **Nice to have** * Familiarity with automated testing, CI/CD pipelines, or build systems. * Experience with modern UI development patterns and maintainable application architecture. * Interest in audio, video, or consumer\-facing technologies. Dolby Hiring Entity: 77 Sir John Rogerson's Quay, Block C Grand Canal Docklands Dublin D02 VK60Ireland

title: Field Service Technician - Talent Pool (Dublin)
description: **About Miele** How does a family\-owned company become the global market leader in the premium segment for domestic appliances and a trusted partner for solutions in commercial and medical technology? By striving to always do better and to keep improving. This mindset defines what it means to work at Miele and drives the success of our products. Our approximately 23,000 employees can rely on one thing: those who embrace our commitment to quality will grow – both personally and professionally. **​** **Talent Pool: Domestic Premium Appliance Field Engineer (Dublin)** **Please note, this is a Talent Pool and not a live vacancy. You are registering your interest in Field Service Technician opportunities in the Dublin area.** *Opportunities will be full\-time (40 hours per week) with the option to work this across 4 or 5 days a week.* The Miele Service team is an award\-winning service delivery function, supporting the needs of our domestic customers directly in their homes. We hire the best technicians from the white goods sector to join our field service community. Our team is built on a legacy of excellence and innovation that dates back to 1899\. We are proud to offer a workplace and culture that is as future proof as our world\-renowned products. At Miele, we don’t just build appliances—we build careers. Join us in living our philosophy of being ‘Immer Besser’—Forever Better. **About the role** Working independently, we give you the tools to succeed. We build a community around you to share your practices and feel part of something special, inspiring results as a team with a family feel, and redefining excellence every day. Our Domestic Field Service teams work creatively to service our customers in their homes, bringing a new challenge every day as you diagnose and solve the issues that arise. You could be servicing any of our world class products to sustain their life and support our sustainability promise. As one of our brand ambassadors, you will be encouraged and rewarded to promote our care product and appliances. we have a reward platform where you can earn points to exchange for personal gift cards or products. **About you** Ideally you will have worked in an Appliance engineer or Appliance technician role however, if you bring technical experience from any industry, what would be advantageous is if it was gained in the electronics, white goods, electrical or gas industry. We are flexible on you background and can offer full training on our appliances. In turn we will support your training to achieve great results in line with our “Immer Besser” philosophy. Alongside your existing skills and experience, we’re looking for engaged team players who will collaborate, innovate and use their passion to drive an exceptional customer experience at every touchpoint. A gas qualification and experience would be highly desirable but not essential . **What to expect from us:** In addition to a strong commitment to your personal development and growth, we can offer you on target earnings of up to €51,396\.25, along with a comprehensive benefits package: • Starting salary of €42,128\.33 which increases incrementally as you gain skills. • Potential to rise within the first 8 months. • A daily lunch allowance included in salary. • Performance related bonus scheme. • Commission for introducing our customers to products, accessories and care collection. • Optional private use of Company Vehicle subject to relevant HMRC rates. • Contributory pension scheme. • Excellent training and support network. • Active personal development plan and opportunities to further your career. • Substantial discount on all company products. If you’re the best at what you do, we want to hear from you. If you can bring energy and pride in your work to the team, this could be the place for you. Please apply below by sharing your CV, answering the screening questions and make sure to let us know of your location. **Please note this is a talent pool only. Should you be identified as suitable for an opportunity we will be in touch. Equally you can set up job alerts on your Miele account for any opportunities.**

title: Finance Administrator
description: **Your new company** A well\-established organisation in the public sector, based in North Dublin, is seeking to hire a Finance Administrator on a part\-time fixed\-term contract (6 months). This is an excellent opportunity to join a purpose\-driven organisation within a collaborative team environment, and the opportunity to gain strong exposure across finance operations. **Your new role** You will be responsible for supporting the day\-to\-day finance function, including maintaining accurate financial records, preparing monthly and quarterly reports, and assisting with budgeting. Duties will include bank payments and reconciliations, payroll support, petty cash reconciliation, and revenue returns. You will also assist with audit preparation, maintain financial systems and databases, and provide administrative and reporting support across the organisation while liaising with internal and external stakeholders. **What you'll need to succeed** You will have prior experience in accounts, finance administration, or a similar role, along with a strong understanding of reconciliations and financial processes. For part\-time arrangements, a requirement of 2 days onsite will apply, along with opportunities for professional development and career progression. **What you need to do now** If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up\-to\-date copy of your CV, or call us now. If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career. **Desired Skills and Experience** You will have prior experience in accounts, finance administration, or a similar role, along with a strong understanding of reconciliations and financial processes. You will be proficient in accounting software QuickBooks and Excel, with excellent attention to detail and organisational ability. Strong communication skills and a proactive, adaptable approach are essential. A relevant qualification or equivalent experience is required, with public or community sector experience considered an advantage. title: Funds Lawyer
description: **OVERVIEW** The Maples Group is a standard bearer in financial and legal services, trusted by many of the world’s largest hedge fund managers, private equity firms and international corporations. Our distinction flows from our carefully curated team: 2,500\+ professionals characterised by tenacity, ethics and exacting excellence. Operating in key financial centres across the Americas, Asia, Europe and the Middle East, our international presence offers a unique springboard for career development and cross\-cultural immersion. Our side\-by\-side financial and legal services are similarly ripe for interdisciplinary learning and growth. The Maples Group looks to add a Dublin\-based Funds Associate to our team and invites eager and qualified candidates to apply. We are committed to diversity, inclusion and equality of opportunity as we attract, retain and develop world\-class talent. **Who We Seek** Our merit\-based culture suits professionals in pursuit of boundless careers and lives. Beyond their acumen, team members are collaborative and conscientious, bringing a healthy sense of drive and purpose to each interaction and to all aspects of their work. **About the Role** This role represents an excellent opportunity for an outstanding funds lawyer to join our market\-leading Irish Funds and Investment Management Group in Dublin and make a significant contribution to our growing practice. The team advises a wide variety of asset managers, investment firms and financial institutions on the full spectrum of Irish regulated and unregulated investment structures. Our scope of work covers all types of public funds (in particular ETFs, other types of UCITS and retail ELTIFs) and alternative/private funds (in particular private credit, hedge and private equity), as well as specialist associated areas such as sustainable investing and digital assets/ tokenisation. The successful candidate will have exposure to high quality matters and will work as part of, and be supported by, a highly successful and collegiate team. The key responsibilities for this role include, but are not limited to, the following: * Advising our predominantly UK, US and European client base on the structuring, establishment and operation of all types Irish fund products * Reviewing transaction documentation from an Irish law perspective across a wide range of structures and jurisdictions * Preparing, drafting and negotiating fund documentation and agreements * Managing transactions end\-to\-end efficiently and effectively * Engaging in business development activities, being active in the promotion of the firm and seeking opportunities for new work and cross\-selling * Working as part of a team, including supervising junior lawyers’ work and taking an active part in sharing knowledge and experience within the team **EXPERIENCE** **What You Bring** Applicants should have between three to five years' post qualification experience as an investment funds lawyer, ideally gained within a top\-tier law firm. It is essential that all candidates have a strong academic background, with excellent written communication and drafting skills. The successful candidates will also have outstanding technical skills, together with the ability to build relationships with clients and colleagues and work well in a team environment. **BENEFITS \& REWARDS** The most enduring professional relationships are reciprocal relationships. The Maples Group prioritises employee health and wellbeing. Depending on your location, we offer a range of benefits, including: * Comprehensive health coverage (medical, dental and optical) * Competitive vacation packages * Educational assistance and professional development programmes * Savings or pension plan * Life insurance * Travel insurance * Global mental wellness programme * Sports clubs and social events **ABOUT MAPLES GROUP** Over five decades, the Maples Group has grown from modest beginnings into one of the world's preeminent professional services firms, offering specialised fiduciary, fund administration, regulatory and compliance, entity formation and management and legal services on the laws of the British Virgin Islands, the Cayman Islands, Ireland, Jersey and Luxembourg. You can learn more about the Maples Group on our corporate website. Experience our culture and our people on our Careers Page or on LinkedIn. Any candidates invited to interview for a role in Maples and Calder, Dublin, will receive a sensory overview of the office, inclusive of sensory maps and accessibility information. Please note that it is the Maples Group standard policy to undertake various background screening checks on all applicants to whom a conditional job offer is made. Except for roles based in the Republic of Ireland, the background checks will include criminal records checks when a conditional job offer is made. If you have a criminal record, it does not mean that your job offer will be automatically withdrawn. The Maples Group will make all job offer decisions on a case\-by\-case basis and will take a number of factors into account, such as the role that you are applying for and the nature and circumstances of the past offence. You will have the opportunity to discuss the matter with us before a decision is made. Further details will be provided at the time any conditional job offer is made. Disclaimer: All personal information collected during the application process will be used for recruitment\-related purposes only. Please refer to our Job Applicant Privacy Notice at maples.com/privacy for details on how we handle personal information relating to job applicants.

title: Graduate Physiotherapist (FIREFIT Physio & Performance)
description: FIREFIT is one of South Dublin's strongest training communities, with 500\+ active members and a multidisciplinary team of 20\+ professionals across full\-time and part\-time roles \- including Physios, Athletic Therapists, Nutritionists, and Strength \& Conditioning coaches. We're built around coaching standards, community, and long\-term progress. FIREFIT Physio \& Performance is our specialised arm, delivering a tailored, personal approach to rehab and performance. We provide 1:1 Physiotherapy, structured rehab plans that integrate with real training, and specialised services including our Masters (Over\-55s), Youth Development pathway, Group Programmes and Runners Strength \& Conditioning / Rehab. This side of the business is scaling quickly \- and being elevated at the same time. We're now hiring a Graduate Physiotherapist \- on either a full\-time or part\-time basis \- who values evidence\-based care, clear communication, and helping people return to training with confidence. **About this role** This is a blended role across coaching \+ clinic, with a clear pathway to build a strong caseload. We're hiring on both a full\-time and part\-time basis \- we'll shape the days and hours around what suits you, and the structure below scales to either. * **General class coaching \+ 1:1 appointments:** You'll coach within our general classes while also delivering 1:1 appointments. Initially coaching on the floor allows you to build trust quickly, meet members consistently, and typically leads to a high number of initial appointments booked. Over time, as your caseload grows, the role progresses into more dedicated clinic hours. * **Specialist group coaching (Physio \& Performance team only):** You'll coach FIREFIT Physio \& Performance sessions such as our Masters Programme, Youth Development Sessions and Runners Strength \& Conditioning / Rehab programmes. These specialised classes are delivered exclusively by the Physio \& Performance team to ensure clinical\-level coaching standards and continuity for clients. * **Own a niche area:** We're keen to support you in developing a specific niche and becoming the go\-to person for it \- for example women's health, golf\-specific rehab/S\&C, youth athletic development, runners strength \& conditioning, or another performance niche aligned with your strengths and passion. * **Weekly CPD \+ mentorship:** Structured weekly CPD with James Greene (Clinic Director), including case discussions, clinical reasoning and clinic/gym based application. **What we're looking for** * Qualified Physiotherapist (CORU registered/eligible) * Strong interest (or proven experience) in MSK assessment, rehabilitation, and strength \& conditioning principles * Excellent communication skills and a high standard of client care * A collaborative, proactive mindset \- comfortable working alongside coaches, clinicians, and support staff **What success looks like at FIREFIT** FIREFIT Physio \& Performance is rapidly growing \- and we're looking for key players. This role will suit someone who wants to push the business forward, contribute to raising standards, and take ownership of specific areas within the clinic. With prime locations and an already brilliant team, there's a genuine opportunity to help grow FIREFIT Physio \& Performance into one of Dublin's leading options for training\-led rehab and performance care. **How to apply** Send your CV and a short note outlining: * Whether you're interested in a full\-time or part\-time role (and, if part\-time, your general availability) * Your qualifications/registration and work experience * The clients you most enjoy working with * Any niche interests you would be interested in developing (such as women's health, running, golf S\&C, youth, performance rehab, etc.) Job Type: Full\-time Pay: Up to €50,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Gym membership * On\-site gym Work Location: In person

title: Graduate Power Apps Specialist- Dublin/ Kildare
description: **Position: Graduate** Power Applications Specialist\- Dublin/ Kildare **Location:** Dublin/Kildare **Company Description** We are a leading engineering services company with a proven track record of high quality, customer focused project delivery for over 130 years. Our core divisions include Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Sprinkler and Fire Protection. Some of the other services we provide include Waste to Energy (BioEnergy), Specialist Lift, Off Site Fabrication, Technical Support Services, Maintenance, Asset Management, BIM (Building Information Modelling) and Geo\-Surveying. We offer competitive salaries, financial support for further training, Continuous Professional Development (CPD) leading to chartered status, performance incentives and the ability to progress your career with a world class engineering contractor. **Overview of the Post** We are seeking a knowledgeable and versatile specialist to enhance our digital solutions using MS Dynamics 365, SharePoint, and Power Apps including Model driven Applications. **Key Responsibilities:** MS Dynamics 365 Management: * Implement and configure MS Dynamics 365 and Power Applications tailored solutions to meet business requirements. * Collaborate with stakeholders to understand needs and translate them into functional specifications. * Provide ongoing support, maintenance, and enhancements to existing Dynamics 365 applications. Power Apps and Model\-Driven Apps: * Develop and customize Power Apps and Model\-Driven Apps to enhance business processes. * Integrate Power Applications with MS Dynamics 365, SharePoint, and other data sources. * Leverage Power Automate to streamline and automate business processes, integrating with Dynamics 365 and other platforms to ensure efficient data flows and task automation. * Ensure all developed solutions have an intuitive user interface (UI) and are optimised for mobile and desktop experiences. Training and Documentation: * Create comprehensive documentation for developed solutions and processes. * Conduct training sessions for end\-users to ensure effective utilization of applications and tools. Collaboration and Problem Solving: * Work closely with IT and business teams to identify areas for improvement and drive technological solutions. * Troubleshoot and resolve issues related to MS Dynamics, SharePoint, and Power Applications in a timely manner. **Qualifications \& Experience:** * Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology, or a related field. * Proven experience with MS Dynamics 365, SharePoint Online, and Power Applications. * Experience of Model\-Driven Applications and their implementation within business environments. * Proficiency in Microsoft Power Automate, Power BI, and Azure services. * Familiarity with JavaScript, HTML, CSS, and other web technologies is a plus. * Excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Strong communication and interpersonal skills. * Ability to work independently and in a team\-based environment. * Relevant Microsoft certifications desirable (e.g., Dynamics 365, Power Platform). * Experience with Azure DevOps or similar project management tools. * Knowledge of data integration techniques and APIs. * Understanding of information technology fundamental tools and concepts (SDLC) * Strong knowledge of programming languages such as Java or Python. * Experience with database management systems. * Ability to analyze and create tests and develop specifications and requirements for end users to follow. * Ensure all solutions comply with security best practices and relevant regulations (e.g., GDPR) when handling sensitive business data. **Benefits** We offer a competitive salary package commensurate with experience and qualifications. In addition, you will have the opportunity to work in a dynamic and supportive environment, collaborating with industry leaders and contributing to a sustainable future. Our Company values work\-life balance and encourages professional growth through training and development programs.

title: Graduate Training Programme - Hotel Management
description: **Overview:** The MHL Hotel Collection is a collection of unique and individual hotels in key city and resort locations throughout Ireland. We are seeking enthusiastic and driven recent graduates to join our hotel teams. The graduate program is designed to provide you with a comprehensive introduction to hospitality and our group through a structured rotational experience. Upon completion of our programme, it is expected that you will be equipped to pursue a management position within the group. **What to expect:** Over an 18month period you will build relationships with colleagues to support your professional development. There will be rotation across not only departments but also within our 13 hotels to enable you to enhance your understanding of the group, and to support your own leadership journey. You will gain invaluable hands\-on experience within the departments, work alongside experienced managers and be assigned work projects that demonstrate your problem solving and analytical abilities. You will benefit from the guidance of a mentor who is assigned to you at the start of the programme, there will be regular check in's and peer meetings, and both internal and external training will be provided. **What we are looking for:** * Due to graduate or recently graduated with a relevant degree (Hospitality or Tourism \- however if you do not have a degree in these fields, but have experience in hospitality, we would still love to hear from you). * Excellent communication, teamwork and interpersonal skills * Ability to adapt to a fast paced environment * Passion for hospitality, guest satisfaction * Willingness to work flexible hours, including evenings and weekends * Ideally open to travel within Ireland for the hotel rotations **What we offer:** * A competitive graduate salary * Fast track career progression * Employee Assistance Programme * Meals on duty * Employee appreciation and wellbeing week * Employee discounts * Plus more **The programme will commence in September 2026** This is an excellent opportunity for recent graduates to kickstart their careers and develop a strong foundation within the MHL Hotel Collection *Due to the high volume of applications received, we regret that we are only able to contact candidates whose experience and profile closely match the requirements of the role. We sincerely thank all applicants for their interest and for taking the time to apply.* Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent, Graduate Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Ground & Water UK & Ireland - Expression of Interest
description: **Job Description** **What if you could do the kind of work the world needs?** At WSP, you can access our global scale, contribute to landmark projects and connect with the brightest minds in your field to do the best work of your life. You can embrace your curiosity in a culture that celebrates new ideas and diverse perspectives. You can experience a world of opportunity and the chance to shape a career as unique as you To find out more about our growing Ground and Water business click on the following link and discover what awaits you at WSP: Ground risk and remediation \| WSP We are seeking to support our growing world class team to ensure we continue to deliver the high calibre of service and technical excellence both we and our clients expect. With a strong pipeline of confirmed work moving into 2025, we have opportunities for Contaminated Land / Geo\-environmental Consultants and Assistant Consultants to join our team. This role is specifically to support contaminated land assessment and remediation projects. This position offers excellent career development potential including training and mentoring. A competitive salary and benefits package will be provided. **Key Responsibilities** **A little more about your role…** * Site work including supervision and management of site investigations * Field work including soil logging, environmental sampling and ground gas monitoring * Data collation, review and interpretation * Support with site investigation design * Preparation of technical reports including desk studies, site investigation reports, and remediation studies and verification reports * Ensuring that all work is delivered in line with procedures and to the highest safety, health and environmental standards **A Typical Week Could Include Activities Such As** * Phase 1 Desk Study researching and reporting * Phase 2 Site Investigation design, supervision, soil sampling, soil logging, subcontractor management, human health/water environment risk assessment and reporting * Adherence (with training) to best technical, engineering and health \& safety practice * Communicating with clients, subcontractors and working as part of a project team **Your team…** The team provides land contamination management services to private and public sector clients in the property, government agency, energy, industrial and nuclear sectors. WSP provides an excellent environment in which to grow professionally, and the successful candidate will be fully supported with their continued development. The successful candidate will play a key role in the day\-to\-day delivery of projects, working closely with and being supported by team members, project managers and project directors. Our offices have several networks to help early career professionals, meet colleagues and make connections around the business, as well as the opportunity for plenty of social activities. You will be based in the North/North East of England, preferably from our Leeds office or secondly our Newcastle office. You will be expected to attend sites across the UK when required, though primarily in the North of England. **Experience** **What we will be looking for you to demonstrate…** * On\-site experience of site investigation and other similar field works * Experience in the preparation of Phase 1 contaminated land/geo\-environmental desk studies * Knowledge of the requirements for planning, designing and management of site investigations * Experience of soil logging * Knowledge of the Construction Design and Management (CDM) Regulations in site investigations * Knowledge of the land contamination regime within the UK and the risk\-based approach to the assessment of land contamination * Report writing including presenting information in a concise and accurate manner * Data handling, including experience in the use of GIS is desirable **Qualifications** * Relevant degree e.g. chemistry, biology, geology, engineering, or environmental science Don’t quite meet all the criteria? Apply, and we can see how your experience aligns to this role and other opportunities within the team **Imagine a better future for you and a better future for us all.** Join our close\-knit community of talented individuals who share your passion for making a positive impact. Our global team includes more than 69,000 employees, working together to make a difference in communities both close to home and around the world. **With us, you can. Apply today.** **About Us** We are one of the world’s leading engineering and professional services firms. Our 72,800 passionate people are united by the common purpose of creating positive, long\-lasting impacts on the communities we serve through a culture of innovation, integrity, and inclusion. With over 9,000 professionals across the UK and Ireland, we are dedicated to our local communities and propelled by international brainpower. **WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU?** **Work\-life balance** At WSP, we understand that work is just one aspect of your life. It’s important to make time for you, your family, friends, interests and your community. Our hybrid working policy offers the flexibility to work from home two days a week, while also providing opportunities to collaborate in our modern offices across the UK. **Inclusivity \& Belonging** We welcome applicants with varied backgrounds and experiences. We enable rewarding careers by encouraging people to bring their whole and authentic selves to work so that our work represents the fullest spectrum of society. We celebrate integrity and treat people with respect, supporting each other and embracing a culture of inclusion and belonging at WSP. We have our employee resource groups bring together employees and allies with different backgrounds to promote our culture of inclusivity. **Health \& Wellbeing** We are committed to supporting our people, giving you the tools to make improvements to your health and wellbeing through our Thrive programme. Our Virtual GP service gives you access to an NHS or Irish Medical Council GP at a time and place that suits you – giving you peace of mind and quick access to medical advice when you need it most. We also provide reasonable workplace adjustments for those in need. Additionally, you can benefit from the Gymflex scheme, which offers up to 40% off annual gym memberships through our WSP flexible benefits program, as well as a comprehensive menopause support package. **Flex your time** To enhance work\-life balance, WSP offers the “WSP My Hour,” allowing you to take one hour each day for personal activities, with the flexibility to make up the time earlier or later that day. We also provide part\-time and flexible working arrangements, the option to purchase additional leave, and the ability to use your bank holiday entitlement to suit you. **Your development** We understand the importance of development and training to you. That’s why we foster a supportive environment that invests in your growth, whether through training, mentoring, or Chartership. **\#WeAreWSP** *Here at WSP we positively encourage applications from suitably qualified and eligible candidates regardless of sex, race, disability, age, sexual orientation, gender reassignment, religion or belief, marital status, pregnancy or maternity/paternity. As a Disability Confident leader, we will interview all disabled applicants who meet the essential criteria, please let us know if you require any workplace adjustments in support of your application.*

title: Growth Operations Specialist
description: **About Us** How did Web Summit become, in the words of Forbes, “the best tech conference on the planet”? Meaningful connections. Our tech events are unmissable because we make it easier for the right people to meet and connect. Everyone at Web Summit works towards this goal. And we’re just getting started. We’re always looking to build on the impact we have already made at Web Summit. In the coming years we’ll take Web Summit to new markets, promoting global connectivity, highlighting important issues and connecting global leaders – all while making a positive impact on the environment and communities we encounter. To build a better company, we have to better ourselves. We do that by finding the most ambitious people to work with us. **About The Team** The Growth team is at the intersection of marketing, operations and data science. We sit in Marketing \& Analytics, and work with every team in the company to help them achieve their objectives. The end goal of everything we do is to help the company and all the events we run grow at an accelerated rate using a data\-driven, technically\-orientated approach. Our north star is growing numbers and improving processes all across the company. **What You'll Do** * Analyse \& Advise Identify inefficiencies across our GTM and marketing ops workflows and recommend data\-driven improvements * Build \& Automate Develop lightweight and heavy automations and integrations using Python, APIs, and automation tools (Matomo, Zapier, Pipedream, etc) to reduce manual effort and improve data flows * Own CRM Quality Work within Salesforce to manage data integrity, build workflows, and support the broader revenue and marketing teams * Bridge the Gap Act as the connective tissue between technical engineers and non\-technical marketing or commercial stakeholders, confidently translating in both directions * Leverage AI Tooling Use LLM\-powered tools (including Claude Code and agentic workflows) to accelerate analysis, automate tasks, and explore new capabilities * Evaluate \& Adopt Continuously assess new tools and technologies, with a genuine appetite for improving our tech stack. * Collaborate \& Communicate Contribute actively in cross\-functional projects, producing clear documentation, presentations, and recommendations for varied audiences **What We're Looking For** **Technical Skills** * Proficiency in Python for data manipulation, scripting, and light automation * Strong SQL skills for querying and analysing data across relational databases * Hands\-on experience with Salesforce or equivalent CRM platforms * Comfortable working with REST APIs (authentication, pagination, data ingestion) * Experience with automation platforms such as Matomo, Zapier, Pipedream, etc * Exposure to LLMs and AI tooling , prompt engineering, agentic workflows, or tools like Claude Code is a strong plus **Soft Skills \& Mindset** * Excellent verbal and written communication skills , able to adapt your style for technical and non\-technical audiences alike * Strong interest in marketing, business, or partnerships * Demonstrated ability to learn quickly and adapt in a fast\-moving environment * Curious and self\-directed , you actively seek out better ways of doing things * Comfortable with ambiguity and able to define structure where little exists * A natural collaborator who thrives in cross\-functional team environments * Detail\-oriented without losing sight of the bigger commercial picture **Skills We Like To See** * 2\+ years in a Business Analyst, Marketing Operations, Revenue Operations, or similar hybrid technical/business role * Experience working within or alongside a marketing, growth, or GTM function is a significant advantage, candidates who have operated inside a commercial or go\-to\-market team will ramp up faster and add immediate value **Benefits And Perks** * Subsidised healthcare, dental, employee assistance programme (EAP) and more. * Employer pension contribution up to 6% for Irish\-based employees * A dedicated in\-house L\&D department, with access to workshops, online learning and resources to help you excel in your career development. * Company MacBook, generous annual leave and flexible working arrangements. * Our very own Tramway Team, which drives community and morale\-boosting events for employees to participate in. * Annual company get\-togethers, charity days, and monthly wellbeing talks. * Wellness subsidy issued to all employees of €1000 per year **At Web Summit, we embrace diversity and inclusion. We want talent from all walks of life to help us on our journey to digitise the conference sector. Hiring decisions are made purely on experience, skills and the needs of the business.** We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.
