title: Human Resources Business Partner
description: **Role Overview** Our client, a maturing and innovative biotech company, with their headquarters in Dublin, is seeking an HR Business Partner to work closely with the Chief People Officer (CPO) in shaping and delivering a high\-impact people strategy. This is a broad, hands\-on HRBP role operating in a hybrid environment, supporting a growing, science\-led organisation through its next phase of scale. The position will span strategic business partnering alongside operational HR delivery, including recruitment, onboarding, and offboarding. The successful candidate will be commercially aware, pragmatic, and comfortable balancing strategic input with day\-to\-day execution in a fast\-paced, evolving environment. **Key Responsibilities** **Business Partnering \& People Strategy** * Partner closely with the CPO and senior leaders to align people strategy with business objectives. * Provide coaching and support to managers on performance, engagement, and team effectiveness. * Support organisational design, workforce planning, and scaling initiatives. * Contribute to building a high\-performance, values\-driven culture. **Talent Acquisition \& Workforce Growth** * Lead and support end\-to\-end recruitment processes across technical and corporate roles. * Work with hiring managers to define role requirements, sourcing strategies, and interview processes. * Enhance employer branding and ensure a positive candidate experience. **Onboarding \& Employee Experience** * Design and deliver effective onboarding processes to ensure successful employee integration. * Partner with managers to ensure clear role expectations and early performance alignment. * Drive initiatives to improve employee engagement and retention. **Employee Relations \& HR Operations** * Act as a trusted advisor on employee relations matters, ensuring fair and consistent outcomes. * Support performance management processes, including reviews, development planning, and performance improvement cases. * Ensure HR policies and procedures are compliant with Irish employment legislation and best practice. **Offboarding \& Lifecycle Management** * Manage structured offboarding processes, including exit interviews and feedback analysis. * Provide insight into attrition trends and recommend retention strategies. * Ensure compliance and positive employer brand is maintained through employee departures. **Process Improvement \& HR Projects** * Support the CPO in implementing scalable HR processes and systems. * Contribute to HR analytics, reporting, and insights to inform decision\-making. * Participate in key HR initiatives such as performance frameworks, DEI, and leadership development. **Candidate Profile** **Experience \& Qualifications** * Proven experience in an HR Business Partner or Senior HR Generalist role, ideally within biotech, life sciences, tech, or a scaling organisation. * Strong working knowledge of Irish employment law and HR best practices. * Experience supporting recruitment and talent lifecycle activities end\-to\-end. * Involvement in a fast\-paced, growth\-oriented or changing environment. * Relevant HR qualification (CIPD or equivalent) preferred. **Key Characteristics** * Strong interpersonal and influencing skills, with the ability to build credibility at all levels. * Commercially aware with a pragmatic, solutions\-focused approach. * Comfortable operating both strategically and operationally. * Highly organised with strong attention to detail. * Proactive, adaptable, and resilient in a dynamic environment. * Collaborative mindset with a strong sense of ownership. **Why Apply?** * Join a leading\-edge biotech company at an exciting stage of growth and maturity * Work directly with a CPO and leadership team to shape the people agenda * Broad, impactful role with exposure to both strategy and execution * Competitive compensation and long\-term career development potential

title: IT Business Analyst – Systems/Applications
description: We are seeking a Technical Business Analyst that will support the delivery, maintenance, and continuous improvement of the client’s business systems and applications. Thie ideal candidate will have experince working in ***systems delivery, change, or project environments.*** The successful candidate will work closely with internal teams, external partners, and the wider dealer network to ensure high quality system performance and project delivery. **Key Responsibilities** **Systems and Application** * Support integrations, API based services, and cloud hosted applications * Work with third party development partners on requirements, documentation, testing, and release preparation * Assist with version control, code reviews, and system documentation * Develop internal tools or utilities to improve operational efficiency **Helpdesk Escalations and Technical Support** * Serve as an escalation point for complex systems, software, and workflow issues * Provide hands on troubleshooting and root cause analysis for application related incidents * Support the helpdesk team during peak periods and critical incidents * Collaborate with developers and vendors to resolve defects and system errors * Assist internal and external users during planned updates or system rollouts **Project Delivery and Governance** * Deliver technology and software projects using structured project management approaches * Gather requirements, define project scope, and document business processes * Coordinate with internal stakeholders, external suppliers, and business teams * Monitor project risks, progress, and communication activities * Support change management and adoption of new features or systems **Software Quality Assurance** * Carry out functional, regression, and integration testing * Log issues with clarity, prioritise them appropriately, and follow through to closure * Support user acceptance testing and gather feedback from business users * Maintain test plans, acceptance criteria, and quality documentation **Artificial Intelligence and Automation** * Use AI tools to support documentation, analysis, troubleshooting, and development tasks * Identify opportunities for AI driven automation within business systems and support processes * Assist staff in the safe and effective use of AI technologies * Contribute to AI related initiatives across internal systems and operational workflows **Required Skills And Experience** * A relevant third level degree in IT, Computer Science, or a related field. * Two to five years’ experience as a Business Analyst in a software, systems, or application focused IT role * Strong troubleshooting and problem\-solving ability * Experience with system integrations and APIs * Understanding of the software development lifecycle and release management * Experience supporting or maintaining business applications * Strong communication skills and ability to work with both technical and non\-technical users * Ability to manage workload across development, support, and project activities * Experience working with third party development partners * Strong team player **Preferred Qualifications And Experience** * PRINCE2, Agile, Scrum, or similar project management certification * Experience with modern CRM Systems (Salesforce, MS Dynamics, others) * Experience with scripting, automation, data workflows, and minor feature enhancements * Practical experience using AI tools for automation, analysis, or workflow improvement Apply Now

title: IT Operations Engineer
description: **What’s the Opportunity** We are seeking a skilled and proactive IT Operations Engineer to join our team. In this critical role, you will be instrumental in delivering IT support to Distilled’s Employees enabling a seamless hybrid working environment and ensuring everyone has the tools and support needed to do their best work, while maintaining the smooth operation and continuous improvement of our internal IT systems. You'll leverage your expertise to resolve complex technical challenges, contribute to strategic IT initiatives and work with a diverse range of cutting\-edge technologies that drive our company's success. This is an exciting opportunity to deepen your technical knowledge, enhance our operational efficiency, and play a key role in scaling our IT environment. **Location:** This role requires working from our Dublin office 4 days per week. **What will I be doing** As an IT Operations Engineer, you, along with the existing ITOps team, will be responsible for a wide range of technical operations, ensuring the delivery of dependable and resilient IT services. The ideal candidate can balance reactive support—managing urgent incoming issues—with proactive work such as scheduled maintenance and strategic improvements, while consistently looking for opportunities to optimize systems \& processes and to ensure that our employees have the tools to do their best work. * **IT Support \& Service Delivery:** Deliver comprehensive remote and onsite IT support through the ticketing system for approximately 250 employees across multiple locations and countries, with a strong focus on providing an excellent service experience for all users. Responsibilities include troubleshooting hardware, software, and system issues, managing equipment lifecycle and procurement, and overseeing the user lifecycle, including onboarding and offboarding. The role also requires a proactive approach to anticipating user needs and continuously improving the support experience. * **Infrastructure Administration:** Administer, monitor and optimize critical IT infrastructure, including networking, firewalls, video conferencing and AV systems, ensuring performance, security and high availability. Manage and secure remote devices via Jamf, Intune and Google Workspace * **Identity \& Access Management:** Manage user accounts and permissions within Okta/Active Directory, encompassing security policy and MFA administration. Optimise the IT aspects of employee onboarding and offboarding processes. * **Operational Excellence \& Documentation:** Drive continuous improvement initiatives for IT operations. Create and maintain comprehensive IT documentation. Co\-manage IT asset inventory and refine escalation processes. * **Project Contribution \& Event Support:** Contribute to the planning and implementation phases of IT projects with a focus on innovation, automation and security. Provide technical support for company\-wide initiatives and events. **What skills and experience do I need** * At least 3 years experience in an IT Operations or System Administration role providing hands on support for employees. * Extensive enterprise experience administering and troubleshooting macOS, Windows, and mobile devices, with strong expertise in MDM solutions including Intune, Jamf and Google Workspace. * Proven expertise in administering and supporting Google Workspace or Office 365\. * Hands\-on experience with administering Cloud Computing platforms (AWS/Azure/ Google Cloud), including IaaS and PaaS components. * Experience in managing and troubleshooting enterprise\-level Video Conferencing systems. * Solid understanding and practical experience with Networking concepts (VPNs, Firewalls, Wi\-Fi, VLANs, routing, switching). * Experience with Identity Management platforms (e.g., Okta, Azure AD Identity Protection, SSO integration). * GDPR and information security focused. * A proactive, adaptable professional with outstanding interpersonal and communication skills, as well as a technical and problem\-solving mindset. **Advantage if you have:** * Experience with scripting languages for automation and system administration. * Familiarity with IT security best practices and incident response. * Industry recognised credentials: Microsoft Azure/Endpoint/SecOps, AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner, Okta Professional/Administrator, Jamf 100/170/300 , Associate Google Workspace Administrator, Comptia Security\+, ITIL Foundation. * Experience in upcoming AI trends in predictive security, agentic AI for automation and specialized IT service management (ITSM) to handle AI\-driven threats **What’s it like to work here** You will have the opportunity to work with one of Ireland's fastest growing Internet companies. You will get the chance to create a significant contribution to our strategic direction. Projects are exciting and employees are given wide scope for creativity and innovation **Benefits \- what you can expect** * Our goal is to maintain a culture where people look forward to work – whether in our centrally located office in Dublin or working from home. * We do that by making Distilled a place you'll be listened to and trusted to do your job. Where you can have fun, learn and feel appreciated. And where you get focused career development, growth and progression opportunities. * We want you to feel confident that this is the right place for you, your career, your wellbeing and your future. * Above all, your health and wellbeing are at the forefront of how we build the business. That means looking after you while you're here and later in life. So you'll receive life insurance, fully\-paid healthcare and a 5% contribution towards your pension. **More about Distilled** We exist to make buying and selling in Ireland simple. We put our people first, creating an inclusive environment where we can develop products that put our users first. We value people’s work and time, creating a community where they have an opportunity to make an impact and grow. Our challenge is to enhance our market\-leading products using insights and empathy to present our users with the perfect match. What we build is technology, but what we do is create connections for life’s most meaningful moments. We are Distilled and we **bring people together** . **Our Values** We have five core values: We have a **growth mindset.** We like to **simplify things** and **make things happen** . We **collaborate** across teams, business units and brands. We're **passionate** about what we do. These values give us a framework for leadership and daily decision making. They embody how we behave and present ourselves — as individuals and as a business — both internally and externally for our colleagues, our users and our customers. *We are a diverse bunch of people here in Distilled, and we want to continue to attract and retain a diverse range of people into our Company. We do not discriminate based on gender, marital status, family status, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, race and membership of the Traveller community.*

title: IT Systems Engineer
description: **About the Company** We are seeking an experienced IT Systems Engineer to support and maintain a modern hybrid IT environment. This is a hands\-on role focused on identity and access management, endpoint administration, SaaS application support, and day\-to\-day IT operations. **About the Role** The successful candidate will work closely with internal teams to ensure systems remain secure, reliable, and efficient while supporting users across a growing organisation. **Responsibilities** * Manage user accounts, permissions, and access across Microsoft 365, Entra ID, Google Workspace, and business applications. * Administer endpoint management platforms, including device enrolment, software deployment, and security policies. * Support Single Sign\-On (SSO), identity federation, and authentication services across SaaS applications. * Manage user onboarding and offboarding processes. * Provide 1st and 2nd line IT support for hardware, software, access, and security\-related issues. * Support VPN, password management, and remote access solutions. * Maintain and improve automation, workflows, and integrations across cloud platforms. * Monitor and respond to security alerts, phishing incidents, and access\-related issues. * Create and maintain technical documentation, procedures, and user guides. * Identify opportunities to improve processes, reduce manual effort, and enhance the user experience. **Qualifications** * Strong Microsoft 365 and Entra ID (Azure AD) administration experience. * Experience administering Google Workspace in a hybrid environment. * Knowledge of SSO technologies and identity federation (SAML, OIDC). * Experience with endpoint management solutions such as Microsoft Intune and other MDM platforms. * Experience supporting Windows and Mac devices. * Strong troubleshooting skills across infrastructure, applications, networking, and authentication. * Experience supporting users in a fast\-paced business environment. * Scripting and automation experience using PowerShell, Python, or similar tools. * Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills. **Required Skills** * Experience supporting cloud\-first environments. * Knowledge of compliance and security frameworks. * Experience supporting organisations through IT migrations or transformation projects. * Familiarity with API integrations and automation tools. **Preferred Skills** Ideal Candidate: This role would suit a Systems Engineer, Infrastructure Engineer, Modern Workplace Engineer, IT Operations Engineer, or Senior IT Support Engineer who enjoys a mix of hands\-on support, cloud administration, identity management, and process improvement.

title: Information Technology Service Engineer
description: Sysco are currently recruiting for a **onsite Services Engineer** to join the **Dublin** , Ireland Technology team reporting to the Services Engineer Manager. This position will be responsible for delivering Sysco technology services support located at sites within their designated region. The primary responsibilities of this position are related to the ongoing support of technology in the field to include, but not limited to workstations, output devices, (Printers and Multifunction Printers), software license compliance, security compliance, and hands\-on support of IT infrastructure in the field in an overall objective to ensure a consistent technology experience across their region. **Key Accountabilities \& Responsibilities:** * Provide technical support to Sysco operating sites across the designated region. * Ensure adherence to IT standards and provide the highest level of overall customer satisfaction. * Assist in ensuring accurate inventory of IT assets across their region. * Responsible for keeping business and IT management informed and escalating impactful issues on a timely basis as required. * Maintain accurate asset inventory of IT hardware, including but not limited to Laptops, Desktops, Printers, Networking hardware including switches, routers and access points, mobile phones, tablet devices, UPS and Comms Room Air Conditioning * Maintains documentation of support related issues. * Responsible for their continual improvement including uptake of training opportunities for professional and career development. * Participate in Agile daily standups, backlog and work in progress. **About you:** To be successful in this role you will have considerable customer service experience in Computer Technology and be proficient at technical troubleshooting of desktop and laptop computers, mobile technology devices, Microsoft Windows, Office 365, MacOS, VMWare Workspace One and other desktop software. You will be able to use remote support tools to be able to troubleshoot remote computer devices and must be available for after hour support and weekend on\-call support as needed. You will be a resourceful, reliable and trustworthy person who’s accurate with numbers and able to use mathematical formulas within spreadsheets with excellent communication skills, (both written and verbal), organisational, presentation and problem\-solving skills.

title: Information Technology Support Analyst
description: Our Dublin based client are looking for an **Information Technology Support Analyst** to join their team on a permanent basis You will have the opportunity to become part of an established and strong team and work for a company that is a market leader in their field There is an on site presence required for this role and the office is Dublin city centre based **Responsibilities:** * Provide technical support to end\-users and assist in resolving various IT issues in an effective and timely manner. * Learn to identify and resolve IT problems and issues as they arise * Troubleshoot hardware and software issues on site and via cloud services * Assist in the setup, configuration, and maintenance of end\-user devices, laptops and phones * Maintain a highly organised inventory of IT assets and resources (ITAM). * Assist in monitoring and tracking issues to prevent problems occurring a second or third time. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to ensure escalations and tickets are registered and dealt with effectively. * Contribute to IT projects, including software deployments and hardware upgrades. * Escalation of technical issues to other team members when necessary and required **Skills and Requirements:** * Minimum of 2\-3 years of experience in a technical support role. * Experience with Active Directory, Azure AD, and managing security groups. * Ability to handle access requests and managing permissions. * Proven capabilities in troubleshooting Office 365 issues and software installations. * Proactive problem\-solving skills. * Have strong verbal and written communication skills. * Demonstrated teamwork skills, attention to detail, and a strong work ethic. * Enthusiasm for technology and a desire to learn and grow in the field. **HOW TO APPLY:** To apply for this role, please submit your updated CV. If your skills and experience match job requirements, you will be contacted to discuss your application in detail. **SIMILAR ROLES OPEN:** As a specialized recruitment agency, we also have several other permanent and contract IT roles available. title: Information Technology Support Engineer
description: Excellent job opportunity for an IT Support Engineer with a dynamic and innovative company provide EPOS, dispensary and 3rd party ordering systems to clients in order to improve business model and optimise operations with increased efficiency and speed of delivery for customers. This role is a hybrid work model with office based in Dublin. **Key responsibilities** * Providing product support to customers across Ireland. * Liaising staff out in the field to address client product issues or EPOS issues in\-store. * To cross train into other areas of customer service as required * Foster excellent relations with all customers and to all new and existing customers. * Log and manage all customer queries through the Magna CRM system (based on Microsoft Dynamics365\). * Setting up new customers on product EPOS and ordering systems. * Assist with data migrations from various dispensary software systems, which may be required outside of typical business hours from time to time. **Work activities include:** * Logging incoming customer technical issues, take ownership to root causing the problem and work on resolving each issue. * Communicating by phone with customers on technical issues and supporting them to resolve all issues. * Communicate with third party technical vendors for third level support * Occasional on\-site visits to customers to resolve problems that could not be addressed over the phone. * Site visits to support with the installation of equipment and software prior to training day. Training will be provided where necessary. **Desirable requirements of the role** * 2\-3 years’ experience of providing IT support on web\-based or Windows\-based applications * Experience of working for an IT company that supports retail customers. * Experience of managing a web\-based system across a wide range of customers, fixing issues both on\-site and remotely. * Experience of Microsoft Dynamics365 CRM system for managing customer experience. title: Junior Quantity Surveyor
description: **Job Description** At IPS, we are global leaders in developing innovative solutions for the consulting, architecture, engineering, project controls, procurement, construction management, and CQV of technically complex facilities, primarily in the life sciences industry. We are looking for a talented **Junior Quantity Surveyor** to join our growing construction team to be based at our EMEA office located in **Dublin, Ireland** . We are looking to appoint a construction industry professional with expert knowledge of construction costs and contracts. In this role, you will be responsible for managing all aspects of the contractual and financial side of construction projects and ensuring that construction projects are completed within their projected budget. The Quantity Surveyor assists with the valuation of construction works, bidding and project budgeting, and the submission of bills to the client, in addition to estimating services to projects and business development efforts. **Job Duties And Responsibilities** * Prepares service estimates for projects as required to support the achievement of corporate objectives * Prepares constructed cost estimates for projects as required to support achievement of corporate objectives * Supports the design, implementation, and maintenance of estimating programs and systems for the corporation * Works with Project Executives and Project Managers to coordinate the acquisition of input information for the estimating process for projects * Prepares project estimates using drawings, specifications, scope, basis of design, and other project information as input * Maintains files of project estimates and supporting backup documentation for reference during project execution * Supports the evaluation of historical data, including purchase orders, subcontracts, force reports, and productivity reports, and incorporates relevant data into the company unit cost, material, and labor factors * Monitors subcontract and purchase order awards, compares awarded costs to budgeted costs, determines the bases for variances, and adjusts estimating systems, models, and unit pricing to adjust future estimates * Reviews design options and recommends the best solutions based on cost, engineering quality, or availability of materials * Maintains relationships in the industry to facilitate the acquisition of estimating data and processes * Prepares estimates to support the change management process for projects * Works with Project Executives and Project Managers to monitor project implementation for compliance with the basis of the project estimate as required * Evaluates required estimate contents by reviewing requests for proposal, attending pre\-bid meetings, and communicating with proposal team to support preparation of bids and proposals during bid/proposal preparation phase * Special projects as assigned **Qualifications \& Requirements** * Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Construction Management, or a related discipline, or an equivalent technical degree. * Significant experience in Project Delivery, including Estimating and Cost. * 3\-6\+ years relevant industry experience. * Exposure to MEP and Cost Estimating systems is a distinct advantage. **Skills** * Written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to write clear reports to convey complex information simply to a diverse range of people. * Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to build and develop relationships. * Numerical and data analysis skills. * A creative and innovative approach to problem\-solving. * IT skills and the ability to learn sophisticated design and costing IT packages. * Teamwork skills and the ability to motivate and lead those on\-site. * Project management skills. * Resilience, determination, and the ability to work well under pressure. * Good commercial awareness. * A practical, logical, and methodical approach to work. * Attention to detail. * Detailed knowledge of past and current building and construction technology, processes, materials, business, and legal matters. All interviews are conducted either in person or virtually, with **video required** . **About Us** **IPS** , a Berkshire Hathaway company, is a global leader in technical consulting, architecture, engineering, procurement, construction management, commissioning, qualification, and validation services for technically complex facilities. **Integration** is how we think and work. It requires the ability to unite existing and emerging technologies into a process that reliably delivers products and accelerates speed to market. That end\-to\-end mindset allows us to be agile and anticipate challenges as we move projects toward operational readiness. IPS applies design and delivery expertise, client\-side experience, and supplier\-side insight to every project. We infuse our curiosity, our passion, and our commitment to partnership in delivering solutions at any scale. We meet our clients where they are and get them to where they need to go. That’s only possible when we care as much as our clients do. Specialties Process Architecture, Process Design, Engineering, Construction, Commissioning, Qualification, LEAN Project Delivery, Strategic Master Planning, Energy Conservation Management, Project Controls, Program/Project Management, Operations/Logistics Strategy and Planning, EPCMV, and Staffing Services. *All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment at IPS without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, sex, age, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetic predisposition or carrier status, veteran, marital, or citizenship status, or any other status protected by law. IPS is proud to be an affirmative action and equal opportunity employer.* Connect With Us! If you do not meet the qualifications for this specific role, but would like to submit your resume for general consideration, please click here!

Our breadth, depth, insights, facilities and logistical expertise give us unparalleled capabilities to meet needs for food, beverages, health and wellness, and more. From the seed of the idea to the outcome of the solution, we enrich the quality of life the world over. Learn more at www.adm.com.

Originally established in New York City and infused with the vibrant spirit of Americana, to create the modern uniform of accessible luxury. The TOMMY HILFIGER brands consist of TOMMY HILFIGER and TOMMY JEANS, complemented by a range of licensed product categories that extend the brand lifestyle across generations, geographies, activities and occasions. Tommy Hilfiger’s global marketing approach and communications strategy taps into culture through the world of F.A.M.E.S.: the people, organizations and ideas boldly driving the future of Fashion, Art, Music, Entertainment and Sport. Major global campaigns, sponsorship platforms, and disruptive activations with world\-class athletes, musicians and entertainers bring a constant source of energy and inspiration to the brand. Immerse yourself in TOMMY HILFIGER here! (**YouTube**, **Instagram**, **TikTok**) Tommy Hilfiger is one of the world’s leading designer lifestyle brands, internationally recognized for celebrating the essence of “classic American cool” style. Founded in 1985, Tommy Hilfiger delivers premium styling, quality and value to consumers worldwide with a breadth of collections including Hilfiger Collection, Tommy Hilfiger Tailored, men’s, women’s and kids’ sportswear, Tommy Hilfiger Denim, accessories, and footwear. In addition, the brand is licensed for a range of products, including fragrances, eyewear, watches and home furnishings. Tommy Hilfiger, which was acquired by PVH Corp. in 2010, has more than 17,000 associates worldwide and an extensive distribution network in over 115 countries and 1,600 retail stores. Global retail sales of the Tommy Hilfiger brand were US $6\.5 billion in 2015\. ***About*** **THE ROLE** When it comes to shopping at Tommy Hilfiger, our customers only deserve the very best in store experience we can offer. You will be working as part of our store team in a luxury retail environment where you will share a passion for our brand and our values with our customers and colleagues. Leading by example and putting all the PVH Values to practice are also of importance in this role. You will be inspiring your team to generate sales \& profit, minimize losses, and create the best possible customer experience whilst ensuring the store is visually distinctive \& impeccably maintained. Responsibilities include: * Be able to demonstrate in\-depth product knowledge and possess successful link selling skills to drive business. * Holding the ability to outfit build for customers will be essential, our Sales Associates should also be able to make further product recommendations and drive our store KPI’s. * Building and maintaining professional relationships with our customers, to secure regular clienteles is possible. * Being a Brand Ambassador and maintaining a keen interest in current fashion and market trends. * Ensuring shop and stock room maintenance, presentation and organization issues are addressed in an appropriate manner. * Show flexibility and innovation when reacting to the changing business environment especially around peak trade. ***About*** **YOU** * Have previous experience within hospitality/retail * Be hardworking with great communication skills * Have an interest in Fashion/Retail * Ability to work in a fast paced environment with ability to multi task in high pressure environments * Be passionate to drive sales using our digital platforms! ***About*** **WHAT WE OFFER** At PVH, we understand that the success of our organisation is directly related to our hardworking and dedicated associates who contribute their time and talents to help make our Company the success it is. That is why we are committed to providing a competitive and comprehensive benefits program that offers the protection, peace of mind and flexibility designed to support our associates – both at home and at work. PVH Europe and our brands, TOMMY HILFIGER and CALVIN KLEIN, are committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse community of associates with a strong sense of belonging. We believe we are bettered by all forms of diversity, and take pride in working with top talent from every walk of life and corner of the world. In the spirit of inclusivity, qualified applicants will be considered without regard to age, ethnicity, ability, gender, gender expression, gender identity, nationality, race, religion or sexual orientation. **About PVH:** We are brand builders who focus our passion and creativity to build Calvin Klein and TOMMY HILFIGER into the most desirable lifestyle brands in the world and at the same time position PVH as one of the best\-performing brand groups in our sector. Guided by our values and enabled by our scale and global reach, we are driving fashion forward for good, as one team with one vision and one plan. That’s the Power of Us, that’s the Power of PVH\+. One of PVH’s greatest strengths is our people. Our collective desire is to create a workplace environment where every individual is valued, and every voice is heard, and we are committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse community of associates with a strong sense of belonging. Learn more about Inclusion \& Diversity at PVH **here**. *PVH Corp. or its subsidiary ("PVH") is an equal opportunity employer and considers all applicants for employment on the basis of their individual capabilities and qualifications without regard to race, ethnicity, color, sex, gender identity or expression, age, religion, national origin, citizenship status, sexual orientation, genetic information, physical or mental disability, military status or any other characteristic protected under federal, state or local law. In addition to complying with all applicable laws, PVH is also committed to ensuring that all current and future PVH associates are compensated solely on job\-related factors such as skill, ability, educational background, work quality, experience and potential.*

title: Personal Lines Account Executive (Junior to Mid-Level)
description: **Responsibilities** * Providing professional best advice and guidance to clients on the most suitable product to meet their Personal Insurance requirements. * Handling new business enquiries for personal insurance and processing quotations. * Evaluate client requirements and provide suitable cover solutions * Deliver first class care and professional best advice to existing clients during and at renewal stage. * Issuing and amending renewals. * Handling Mid\-Term Adjustments, queries and any administration duties that come with servicing clients insurance needs. * Ensuring all compliance standards are met during all processes **Requirements** * Minimum of 1 year experience in personal insurance enquiries for new or existing customers in Ireland. * Hold an Approved Product Adviser (APA) qualification in Personal General Insurance and/or have made progress towards achieving a CIP qualification.

title: Physiotherapist
description: **Job description:** Join Our Team as a Full\-Time Physiotherapist at The Marlay Nursing Home, CareChoice Are you a dedicated and passionate Physiotherapist seeking an exciting opportunity to make a meaningful impact? Look no further, we're inviting applications for Full\-Time Physiotherapist positions to join our dynamic team. Why Choose CareChoice? At CareChoice, we are committed to revolutionizing the healthcare experience. With a legacy of over two decades in the Nursing Homes Sector, we are embarking on a journey to become the most trusted and respected Nursing Home brand in Ireland. Resident Care * Assess and analyse the effect of illness, disability, injury and inactivity on the functional ability of individual residents. * Assess individual residents with actual or potential impairments or other health\-related conditions using detailed history\-taking, as well as reviewing Care Plans and any other documentation available regarding the residents' condition. * Develop and regularly review an individual treatment plan for each resident that is consistent with the individual's goals, general health status, functional needs and assessment findings and that incorporates the application of selected approaches and techniques supported by the best evidence available. * Implement selected interventions safely to relieve pain; achieve and maintain mobility, health and fitness, functional independence and physical performance; and manage the identified impairments, disabilities and limits to participation. * Utilise exercise, movement, mobilisation and other appropriate treatments in the Nursing Home setting with a view to maximising resident mobility and well\-being. * Assist residents with joint and spinal problems including following surgery. * Assist residents' rehabilitation following accidents, injury and strokes. * Evaluate the health status of each individual resident through the use of appropriate measurable outcomes as a baseline for monitoring or to determine the result, impact of effectiveness of physiotherapy intervention. * Educate and advise residents, their family members and Carechoice staff about how to prevent and/or improve conditions. Management responsibilities Participate in the management of the Physiotherapy service as a member of the Care team in Carechoice in co\-operation with the Director of Nursing. Create and promote good working relationships with colleagues, residents and family members through open communication. Contribute to and participate in staff induction and in\-service training programmes including but not limited to Manual Handling and Patient Moving Training. Monitor staff Manual Handling practices on an ongoing basis and address any shortfalls in staff practices; report issues to Asst. Director of Nursing or Director of Nursing. Actively participate in and promote continuing education and research activities consistent with the position. Maintain appropriate records and statistics sufficient for organisational and legal purposes to include work related injuries/incidents. Input data to the computerised resident care system. Generate regular reports to senior management team Assist in the supervision and education of student and junior physiotherapists and physiotherapy support workers as appropriate. Liaise with other healthcare professionals to exchange information about the background and progress of patients, as well as to refer patients who require other medical attention. Essential Criteria: * Three years of post\-registration experience * A minimum of one years in the care of the elderly * Continuous Professional development * Must be registered with CORU What we offer: * Above market rate salary package * Comprehensive induction training * A rewarding working environment, with support from supervisors and managers * Training \& Development opportunities * Employee Assistance Programme which offers advice and counseling services, for carers and their immediate families * Free parking onsite Job Type: Full\-time Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Employee assistance program Schedule: * Day shift * Monday to Friday * Weekend availability Ability to commute/relocate: * Rathfarnham, Dublin 16, D16 A363: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Education: * Bachelor's (required) Experience: * post reg: 3 years (required) * care of the elderly: 1 year (required) Language: * English (required) Licence/Certification: * CORU Registration (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Product Owner
description: **Tech that takes you places** We’re CarTrawler, the global travel tech company behind seamless connections between people, places and possibilities. From powering car rental to creating smarter ways to move, we make travel smoother for millions worldwide. Our culture is built on curiosity, collaboration and craic where every idea counts and every journey matters. Ready to make an impact? Let’s go places together. Junior Product Owner role We’re looking for a Product Owner with a passion for delivering value who will work with our teams to deliver and configure products for our extensive range of partners. This is an outstanding opportunity for an analytical problem solver to take your product skills to the next level. **Your Journey starts here!** ### **What you’ll do as a Product Owner** * Maximise the value of our products by delivering best\-in\-class integrations, optimising scalability and driving performance of products for existing partners * Clearly articulate and manage requirements, working with stakeholders and the delivery team to break down, outline and refine user stories and acceptance criteria. Have a clear focus on always delivering business value * Take ownership for successful delivery and launch of solutions, collaborating effectively across teams to manage dependencies and ensure successful outcomes * Work closely with Product Managers to understand business and commercial context, priority and support definition of solutions * Work closely with our Go\-Live, Partner Management and Trading Teams to understand our partners technology and business requirements * Collaborate with Product Managers, Designers and Engineering Managers to understand the capabilities of our products and the options for scaling and configuring them. * Producing high quality artefacts related to business rules, requirements, features etc to ensure that information is readily available and clearly communicated to the wider organisation * Identify opportunities for product improvements and support product managers in shaping product roadmap initiatives * Contributing to the continuous improvement of a high performing team that deliver world\-class productS * Identify opportunities for product improvements and support product managers in shaping product roadmap initiatives * Maximise the value of our products by delivering best\-in\-class integrations, optimising scalability and driving performance of products for existing partners ### **What you'll bring to the team** * 3 years' experience as a Product Owner in an agile environment, ideally delivering web and/or API\-driven products within an e\-commerce context. * Agile / Scrum certification (e.g. CSPO, PSPO) is desirable * Strong understanding of lean/agile principles and the full software delivery lifecycle * Experience gathering requirements from external stakeholders/partners and translating them into user stories/acceptance criteria * Strong backlog management experience: refinement, prioritisation, dependency management, release planning * Comfortable using data and KPIs to prioritise work and measure impact (e.g., conversion, performance, quality) * Proven track record of collaborating effectively with engineers, designers, and senior leadership to drive product outcomes * Demonstrated ability to apply both creative and analytical thinking to complex problem\-solving * Experience working across multiple devices and channels to deliver cohesive customer experiences We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Project Control Specialist
description: **Infrastructure Project Controls Specialists** (all levels), \- **Infrastructure \& Capital Projects** \- Business Consulting Location: **Dublin,** Ireland Contract Type: **Full\-Time – Permanent** **The team and the opportunity** To continue the successful growth of our client’s **Infrastructure \& Capital Projects** team within their large **Consulting** practice, are looking for experienced and motivated **Project Controls Specialists** with a background in delivering complex, **High‑Value Capital Programmes.** The successful candidate will be responsible for overseeing and managing all aspects of **Project Controls** within the **Infrastructure Sector** , ensuring projects are delivered on time, within budget, and in line with quality and governance requirements. This role requires meticulous attention to detail, strong analytical capability, and the ability to communicate and collaborate effectively with a broad range of stakeholders. Their **Infrastructure \& Capital Projects** team sits within the **Transformation Delivery** competency of **Irish Consulting practice.** Their team is committed to delivering exceptional client services across a range of sectors including **Energy, Utilities, Transport, Water, Gas, Telecoms, Housing, and Healthcare.** Our ambition is to become the **Trusted Advisor** for our clients in the **Procurement, Construction** , and **Operational Phases** of **Major Capital Programmes** by leveraging: Their people and their extensive industry experience. Existing partnerships and relationships. Their global experience across the **Infrastructure \& Capital** projects lifecycle. **Your key responsibilities** To support the continued growth of their business, they are currently recruiting **Project Controls Specialists** with an **Engineering, Technical** , or **Construction** ‑related background. Work directly with their **infrastructure clients** across our focus sectors ( **Energy, Water, Transport, Gas, Telecoms** , **Housing,** and **Healthcare** ) to develop innovative insights and solutions to meet their needs. Develop and implement **Project Control** processes and procedures to effectively manage **Scope, Schedule, Cost** , and **Quality.** Collaborate with **Project Managers, Engineers, Contractors** , and other stakeholders to establish project baselines and monitor performance against established metrics. Create and maintain detailed **Project Schedules** , including **Critical Path Analysis** and resource allocation, to optimise project timelines and identify potential risks and opportunities. **Monitor Project Costs** and expenditures, track budget variances, and provide timely analysis and reporting to support informed decision\-making. Identify and mitigate **Project Risks** through proactive risk assessment, analysis, and mitigation strategies. Lead or support regular progress meetings and presentations to update **Project Stakeholders** on project status, key milestones, and performance metrics. Utilise **Project Management Systems** and tools to enhance **Project Controls Processes** and enhance project visibility and transparency. **Provide Mentorship** and guidance to **Junior Project Controls** staff, fostering a culture of continuous improvement and professional development within the team. Support the preparation of **Project Proposals, Bids** , and **Cost Estimates** by providing accurate and reliable **Project Controls Data** and **Analysis.** Ensure compliance with relevant industry **Standards, Regulations** , and **Best Practices** in **Project Controls** and **Management.** **Internal Responsibilities** Support **Client Account Management** activities, including **Project Resourcing, Financial Management** , and **Quality and Risk Management.** Build and maintain strong relationships with clients and their colleagues to support business develop. Communicate effectively with **Senior Management** and support the development, management and motivation of high\-performing teams. Develop a strong understanding of their services offerings and proactively identify opportunities to enhance client value. Support team development activities, including: Coaching, and mentoring. Conducting performance reviews and contributing to feedback. Supporting recruitment, retention and training initiatives. Maintaining continuous technical and professional development. Understanding, applying, and communicating workplace policies and procedures **To qualify for the role, you must have:** 3rd level educational qualification in **Engineering, Construction** , or a related field. **Minimum of 5 years** of experience in **Project Controls** or **Project Management** within the **Infrastructure** sector, with a proven track record of **Supporting Or Managing** **Large\-Scale Programmes.** Ideal candidates will have:\- Experience in **Consulting** or similar client facing role. Multidisciplinary experience including **Project Management** and **Engineering Feasibility** and **Design.** **Excellent Stakeholder Management** and communication skills with a track record in **Managing Complex Programmes.** **Strong Analytical** and **Problem\-Solving skills** , with the ability to **Analyse Complex Data Sets** and develop actionable insights. **Excellent Communication** and interpersonal skills, with the ability to effectively collaborate with diverse stakeholders at all levels. **Demonstrated Leadership Capability** with a proactive, delivery‑focused approach. Knowledge of industry\-specific regulations, standards, and best practices in **Infrastructure Development** and **Construction.** Additional professional qualifications, such as **PRINCE2® or PMP** , are desirable.

title: Project Engineer
description: The Writech Group is an internationally recognized leader in Fire Protection Systems, and we are seeking a Project Engineer to join our team. As a Project Engineer, you will be responsible for overseeing the planning, implementation, and tracking of specific engineering projects. You will work closely with other Project Engineers, Technical Design Leads, clients, and other stakeholders to ensure the successful delivery of projects on time and within budget. Responsibilities: * Work with management to develop project plans, timelines, and budgets * Oversee the execution of engineering projects, including managing resources and ensuring project deliverables are met * Ensure that all project activities comply with company policies, procedures, and quality standards * Monitor project progress and make adjustments as needed to ensure project goals are met * Communicate project status to stakeholders, including clients, project team members, and senior management * Manage project risks and issues, identifying and addressing potential problems before they impact project delivery * Coordinate with contractors, subcontractors, and suppliers to ensure timely delivery of materials and equipment * Participate in project design reviews and provide input on technical issues as needed * Provide guidance and mentoring to junior project engineers as needed **Requirements:** * A Degree in Mechancial, Civil or Electrical Enginerring * A Full Drivers License * Expereince in a similar role would be benenficial but not required. **Additional Benefits** * HSF Medical Cash Plan * Discount Health Insurance * PRSA * EAP * Learning \& Development * Career Progression * Company Events * Refer a Friend Bonus Scheme * Additional Annual Leave **Writech is an equal employment opportunities employer.** Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €35,000\.00\-€42,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Employee assistance program * On\-site gym * On\-site parking * Private medical insurance Education: * Bachelor's (required) Experience: * fire sprinkler and fire protection: 2 years (required) * Fire Sprinkler: 2 years (required) Work Location: In person

title: QHSSE / Quality Graduate
description: **DHL Global Forwarding Graduate Programme 2026** **As a Graduate** , you’ll use your talents to create innovative solutions and shape the future across our business. Join a global network of passionate experts – backed by the latest technology, to develop your career to management level. The DGF Graduate Scheme places you in a variety of roles across our international Freight Forwarding business. Throughout the scheme you will be empowered to learn on the job and deliver real results. We offer a supportive culture in a highly engaged workforce and provide investment in your training to ensure that you get the most out of your graduate scheme. **What the scheme looks like:** 24\-month structured scheme * Year 1\- Rotational placements in key departments * Year 2 \- Working on key business projects focused on continuous improvement, digitalisation and change management in the Business Process \& Service Management team. Working with stations on their metrics, data analysis and systems innovations **Skills, Experience \& Educational Qualifications required:** * 2:1 or above degree (predicted or obtained at the time of application) in a related discipline i.e., Quality Management/ Assurance, Validation,Logistics, supply chain, Transport Management, or related area. * Interested in project management, data analysis/metrics and auditing. * Motivated to develop a career in the Quality / Freight Forwarding / Logistics industry while working closely with pharmaceutical customers. * Passionate about enhancing customer experience and continuously improving processes. * Fully mobile to travel within Ireland and able to work at other sites outside Cedar Drive, Dublin to deliver key projects as required. * Clean Irish driving license. * Eligibility to work in Ireland. * Fluent in English. * Attention to detail enthusiastic and open\-minded with a ‘Can\- Do’ attitude. * Confident communicator – able to tailor communication style according to the audience. **What do we offer?** The scheme will initially be a 24\-month Fixed Term Contract (FTC), with the aim that our Graduates move into a permanent position within the business – *subject to a suitable vacancy being available and in line with the selection process for the role.* **What will you get in return?** * Access to a variety of our excellent benefits, including 21 days holiday, bank holidays, service days, pension scheme, gym membership, discounted private healthcare, education assistance programme and family leave supports. * Early responsibility and fast progression. * Experience working within a fast\-paced environment. * Regular performance feedback and personal development planning. * Management Team Mentor and DGF Buddy. * Competitive salary. * You will be working for an accredited Top Employer and a certified Great Place to Work. **Location:** **Dublin Based** – Cedar Drive, Dublin Airport Logistics Park, St. Margarets, Co.Dublin **Hours:** 37\.5 hours per week **What’s Next?** If you would like to be considered for this position, then send us an email with a copy of your CV outlining your experience to date and suitability for the role. We are committed to equal opportunity and reject any forms of discrimination. The basis for employee selection at DHL Group is qualification, performance, skills and experience

title: Quality Assurance Tech I
description: **Job Title: Quality Assurance Technician I** **Job Description** **Fluent proficiency in at least one non\-English language: Dutch required for this role!** You will execute manual and automated test cases, perform cultural and linguistic validation, analyse failures, raise and track defects, and contribute to continuous improvement of testing processes. Working within a defined test strategy and established Standard Operating Procedures, you will collaborate closely with QA Engineers, QA leadership and cross\-functional teams to deliver accurate, natural and culturally relevant customer experiences. You must be fluent in at least one of French, Dutch or German and communicate clearly in English for effective reporting and collaboration. **Responsibilities** * Execute manual test cases in a structured testing environment, strictly following established Standard Operating Procedures and templates across existing components and new features. * Perform cultural and linguistic validation to ensure responses are accurate, natural and appropriate for the target locale, including verifying localisation elements such as date, time, units and currency. * Assess linguistic quality by reviewing language fluency, dialect features, colloquialisms, speech fluidity, pitch, tone and overall cultural relevance of responses. * Run automated test scripts, monitor their execution, review automation failures and analyse root causes to distinguish between genuine defects and environment\-related issues. * Gather and interpret logs, traces and error messages to support debugging and provide clear evidence of issues. * Reproduce defect behaviour across components and features to validate reported issues and to confirm the effectiveness of fixes. * Perform defect verification to ensure that resolved defects no longer reproduce across relevant environments, devices and locales. * Create clear, well\-documented defect reports with detailed steps to reproduce, expected versus actual behaviour, supporting logs and screenshots. * Escalate promptly when test scripts fail repeatedly or when required actions deviate from established Standard Operating Procedures. * Analyse test results and provide clear, concise reporting on test execution status to stakeholders, partner teams and other relevant audiences. * Install and configure software, tools and test environments across multiple platforms, including Mac, Windows, Linux and mobile devices, to support testing activities. * Support regression testing by executing test plans across multiple locales, endpoints and devices to ensure consistent quality over time. * Recommend improvements to Standard Operating Procedures, tools and metrics to increase testing efficiency, coverage and effectiveness. * Collaborate closely with team members and cross\-functional partners to unblock testing, share findings and ensure timely delivery of high\-quality results. * Apply strong attention to detail to follow documented procedures accurately and maintain high standards of quality in all testing activities. * Use productivity tools, such as the Microsoft Office suite, to document findings, track progress and support reporting requirements. * Contribute to quality assurance and quality control activities within a broader quality management system, including accurate data entry and maintenance of testing records. * Leverage research and analytical skills to investigate issues, identify patterns and support data\-driven decisions about product quality. **Essential Skills** * At least 2 years of experience in software quality assurance or testing in a structured testing environment. * Proven experience executing both manual and automated test cases following defined processes and standards. * Fluent proficiency in at least one non\-English language: Dutch, German or French. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English for clear reporting and team collaboration. * experience creating and managing defects using bug tracking tools such as JIRA or similar systems. * Ability to review automation failures, identify root causes and differentiate between real bugs and environment or configuration issues. * Ability to read and interpret logs, traces and error messages for debugging and defect analysis. * experience installing and configuring software and tools across multiple platforms, including Mac, Windows, Linux and mobile devices. * Strong attention to detail and the ability to follow documented procedures accurately and consistently. * Technical aptitude and confidence working with software tools, test environments and device configurations. * Self\-sufficiency in following Standard Operating Procedures, troubleshooting basic issues and escalating appropriately when needed. * Proactive communication skills to keep stakeholders informed of progress, blockers and results without prompting. * Strong sense of ownership and responsibility for the quality and accuracy of test results. * experience with quality assurance, quality control and working within a quality management system. * Competence using the Microsoft Office suite for documentation, reporting and communication. * Accurate data entry skills and the ability to maintain clear, structured records. * Well\-developed research and analytical skills to support investigation and resolution of issues. **Additional Skills \& Qualifications** * experience with cultural and linguistic validation, ensuring that content is appropriate, natural and accurate for specific languages and dialects. * experience testing voice assistants, conversational AI or natural language understanding (NLU) products. * Familiarity with automation frameworks and the ability to run and review automated scripts, for example using Python, Ruby or shell scripts. * experience with internationalisation (i18n) or localisation testing across multiple languages and locales. * Exposure to CI/CD pipelines and a solid understanding of the Software Test Life Cycle (STLC). * experience working in Agile or Scrum environments and contributing to iterative delivery cycles. * Familiarity with smart home and IoT devices such as Echo, Fire TV, Fire Tablet or similar products. * Interest in languages, social or cultural anthropology, consumer product internationalisation and large language models. * Demonstrated bias for action, taking initiative to unblock testing, pick up available work and support team objectives. * Curiosity and a strong desire to learn, develop technical skills, adopt new tools and improve testing practices over time. Location Dublin, Ireland Rate/Salary 160\.00 \- 170\.00 EUR Daily Trading as TEKsystems. Allegis Group Limited. Level 1, The Chase, Carmanhall Road, Sandyford, Dublin 18, Ireland. No. 909257\. TEKsystems is a company within the Allegis Group network of companies (collectively referred to as "Allegis Group"). Aerotek, Aston Carter, EASi, Talentis Solutions, TEKsystems, Stamford Consultants and The Stamford Group are Allegis Group brands. If you apply, your personal data will be processed as described in the Allegis Group Online Privacy Notice available at https://www.allegisgroup.com/en\-gb/privacy\-notices. To access our Online Privacy Notice, which explains what information we may collect, use, share, and store about you, and describes your rights and choices about this, please go to https://www.allegisgroup.com/en\-gb/privacy\-notices. We are part of a global network of companies and as a result, the personal data you provide will be shared within Allegis Group and transferred and processed outside the UK, Switzerland and European Economic Area subject to the protections described in the Allegis Group Online Privacy Notice. We store personal data in the UK, EEA, Switzerland and the USA. If you would like to exercise your privacy rights, please visit the "Contacting Us" section of our Online Privacy Notice at https://www.allegisgroup.com/en\-gb/privacy\-notices for details on how to contact us.
